pattern: Since a fixed array is matched against only four increasing positions, a rolling four-state DP over the second array (one state per position already filled) tracks the best score in a single pass.
hints: Think of it as filling four slots (matched to a[0], a[1], a[2], a[3] in order) as you scan through b left to right, deciding for each b value whether to use it to fill the next open slot.

Keep a running best score for 'the first j slots are filled using some prefix of b', for j = 0..4; when you see a new b value, it can either skip it or use it to advance slot j-1's best score into slot j's.

Process b once, updating the four running best-scores from the last slot backward to the first (so each b value is considered for at most one slot in a given step) -- the answer is the fourth slot's score after the full scan.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, mixed positive and negative choices
statement example, best score is negative
b has the minimum length, every index must be used
all-negative a and b, maximizing means picking least negative values in order
a large early value in b is tempting but pairing it with the wrong a[j] hurts the total
zeros in a neutralize some picks, forcing the DP to look past them
extreme value magnitudes at the constraint bound

stress.description: random a of size 4 and random b of moderate length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    a = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(4)]
    n = max(4, min(500, size))
    b = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [a, b]
